'''Danny Richardson''' (born {{Birth date|1996|9|2|df=y}}) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], on loan from the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also played for the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=24 October 2019}}</ref>

He has previously played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League. He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Leigh Centurions]], and York in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and at Hull KR, [[Huddersfield Giants]], and [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Richardson was born in [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire, England]].

Richardson played junior rugby league for Widnes Moorfield and Halton Farnworth Hornets. He initially signed for the [[Widnes Vikings]] academy system, before joining the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] scholarship side aged 16.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Gareth |date=11 July 2017 |title=Meet Danny Richardson - The talented St Helens half-back who is making the most of his second chance |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meet-danny-richardson-talented-st-10775062 |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=The Mirror}}</ref>

On 25 September 2016, Richardson was named Man of the Match for his performance in the Academy Grand Final victory against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 September 2016 |title=Saints 19s Are Champions |url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/2016/09/25/saints-19s-are-champions/ |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=St Helens RFC}}</ref>

==Career==
===St Helens===
On 9 February 2017, Richardson made his [[Super League]] début for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shepka |first=Phil |date=9 February 2017 |title=Super League: St Helens 6-4 Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/38896656 |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In October 2017, he signed a new three-year deal at St Helens, with the option of a further year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 October 2017 |title=Danny Richardson: St Helens half-back signs new three-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41485027 |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Richardson was ever-present for the Saints in 2018, making 34 appearances and scoring 6 tries, 147 goals and 7 drop goals. His outstanding personal season saw him named in the [[Super League Dream Team#2018|2018 Super League Dream Team]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 October 2018 |title=Super League Dream Team: Seven St Helens players named in 2018 line-up |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45705029 |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

==== Sheffield Eagles (dual registration) ====
In July 2017, having featured just four times in his first season at professional level, Richardson played for [[RFL Championship|Championship]] outfit [[Sheffield Eagles]] through their new [[Rugby League Dual registration|dual registration]] deal with St Helens. In his only appearance, he scored a try and converted seven goals.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2 July 2017 |title=Sheffield 42 - 34 Swinton - Match Report & Highlights |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/sheffield-vs-swinton/46237 |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

==== Leigh Centurions (dual registration) ====
Richardson made 6 appearances for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] at the beginning of the 2019 season, kicking 9 goals.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gordon |first=James |date=16 February 2019 |title=Danny Richardson set to make Leigh debut |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-richardson-set-to-make-leigh-debut |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref name="RLRKC" />

===Castleford Tigers===
In October 2019, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Richardson on a three-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 October 2019 |title=Danny Richardson joins Castleford Tigers from St Helens on three-year deal |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/11838448/danny-richardson-joins-castleford-tigers-from-st-helens-on-three-year-deal |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He was acquired following the departure of [[Luke Gale]], and would partner [[Jake Trueman]] in the halves.<ref name="LRL Cas Signing">{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=18 October 2019 |title=Castleford confirm signing of Danny Richardson |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-confirm-signing-of-danny-richardson |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> Head coach [[Daryl Powell]] said, "He has a sharp rugby mind alongside his ability to kick the ball in a special way both for goal and tactically."<ref name="LRL Cas Signing" />

Richardson made his Castleford debut on 2 February 2020 against the [[Toronto Wolfpack]], converting four goals.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hafez |first=Shamoon |date=2 February 2020 |title=Super League: Toronto Wolfpack 10-28 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51266825 |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first try for the Tigers against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 27 February, for an individual haul of 16 points.<ref>{{Cite web |date=27 February 2020 |title=Super League: Hull Kingston Rovers 8-28 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51550255 |access-date=9 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Richardson made a total of 16 appearances throughout the [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on rugby league#Super League|COVID-disrupted season]], scoring 2 tries, 54 goals and 1 drop goal.

In June 2021, Richardson was made available to Super League clubs for transfer, having been omitted from Castleford's squad for the Challenge Cup semi-final.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=8 June 2021 |title=Danny Richardson made available to Super League clubs in shock Castleford Tigers development |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/danny-richardson-castleford-tigers-exit-20769666 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref>

Richardson suffered a neck fracture in round 1 of the [[2022 Castleford Tigers season|2022 season]], ruling him out until June.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=9 August 2022 |title=Danny Richardson explains why he is delighted to be staying with Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/danny-richardson-explains-why-he-is-delighted-to-be-staying-with-castleford-tigers-3799500 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> In round 19, Richardson scored a try, kicked five goals and one drop goal in Castleford's 35–22 victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Laybourn |first=Ian |date=16 July 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers 35 Warrington Wolves 22: 12-man Cas hold off Wire fightback to seal crucial win |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-35-warrington-wolves-22-12-man-cas-hold-off-wire-fightback-to-seal-crucial-win-3771082 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Turner |first=Matt |date=16 July 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers 35 Warrington Wolves 22, updates |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/20284212.castleford-tigers-35-warrington-wolves-22-updates/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Warrington Guardian}}</ref> On 9 August 2022, Richardson signed a two-year contract extension to remain at Castleford until the end of the 2024 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 August 2022 |title=Danny Richardson: Castleford's ex-St Helens half-back signs new two-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62486076 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Weeks later, however, he sustained a torn [[Anterior cruciate ligament injury|ACL]] and [[Medial collateral ligament#Injury|MCL]] against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=31 August 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Lee Radford on Danny Richardson ACL fears and Liam Watts concern |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-lee-radford-on-danny-richardson-acl-fears-and-liam-watts-concern-3825565 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Sternik |first=Sebastian |date=6 February 2023 |title=Radford confirms Richardson blow and provides update on injured duo |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/lee-radford-danny-richardson-injured-castleford |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> This serious injury caused him to miss the remainder of 2022, as well as the entire 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2 February 2023 |title=Castleford blow as Danny Richardson unlikely to play at all in 2023 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-richardson-castleford-tigers-blow |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref name="LRL injury recovery">{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=17 February 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers ace Danny Richardson details his harrowing road to recovery ahead of Super League comeback |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-danny-richardson-details-his-harrowing-road-to-recovery-ahead-of-super-league-comeback |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

After 18 months on the sidelines, Richardson returned to training for [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 pre-season]].<ref name="LRL injury recovery" /> He made his competitive return in round 1 against the [[Wigan Warriors]], kicking two penalty goals.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=17 February 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In May, having lost his place in the starting lineup to [[Rowan Milnes]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=27 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Craig Lingard makes Danny Richardson admission following London Broncos thrashing |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-boss-craig-lingard-makes-danny-richardson-admission-following-london-broncos-thrashing/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> Richardson was allowed to depart on loan.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=9 May 2024 |title=Danny Richardson's Castleford Tigers career over with contract situation outlined |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/danny-richardson-castleford-tigers-contract-29137503 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=McHugh |first=Robert |date=9 May 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss explains star man's exit |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-boss-explains-star-mans-exit-91470/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref>

==== Hull Kingston Rovers (loan) ====
On 9 May 2024, [[Super League]] club [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] announced that they had signed Richardson on a season-long loan deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-05-09 |title=Richardson joins Robins on season-long loan while Reynolds heads to Hull FC |url=https://hullkr.co.uk/news/richardson-joins-robins-on-season-long-loan-reynolds-heads-to-hull-fc |access-date=2024-05-09 |website=hullkr.co.uk |language=en}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 23 August 2024, Richardson signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a permanent basis, joining on a two-year deal from the [[2025 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2025 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 August 2024 |title=Richardson signs permanent Hull KR deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp8nj6g2e7lo |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=23 August 2024 |title=Ex-Castleford Tigers halfback secures Super League deal for 2025 and 2026 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-halfback-secures-super-league-deal-for-2025-and-2026/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

==== Huddersfield Giants (loan) ====
On 6 February 2025, Richardson joined [[Huddersfield Giants]] on an initial two-week loan.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=6 February 2025 |title=Huddersfield Giants ease injury problems with Danny Richardson signing |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-ease-injury-problems-with-danny-richardson-signing-4978347 |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> On 20 February, he was recalled early by Hull KR, having played just one game for the Giants.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=20 February 2025 |title=Danny Richardson returns to Hull KR after just one game with Huddersfield Giants |url=https://www.totalrl.com/danny-richardson-returns-to-hull-kr-after-just-one-game-with-huddersfield-giants/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

==== Salford Red Devils (loan) ====
On 24 June 2025, Richardson signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League on a two-week loan.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=24 June 2025 |title=Hull KR playmaker makes cross-Super League switch as latest loan confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-playmaker-makes-cross-super-league-switch-as-latest-loan-confirmed |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

==== York Knights (loan) ====
On 8 August 2025, Richardson joined [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan until the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ramsey |first=Gabriel |date=8 August 2025 |title=York Knights sign half-back Danny Richardson on loan from Hull KR |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/25376491.york-knights-sign-half-back-danny-richardson-loan-hull-kr/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=The York Press}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Thomson |first=Doug |date=8 August 2025 |title=York Knights take Hull KR player on loan |url=https://www.totalrl.com/york-knights-take-hull-kr-player-on-loan/ |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

=== York Knights ===
On 21 October 2025, York announced the signing of Richardson on a permanent basis, agreeing a one-year deal for 2026.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=21 October 2025 |title=Danny Richardson's first words as permanent York move confirmed following Hull KR departure |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-richardsons-first-words-as-permanent-york-move-confirmed |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Widnes Vikings (loan) ===
On 11 June 2026, it was reported that Richardson had signed for hometown club [[Widnes Vikings]] on loan until the end of the 2026 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Matthew |date=11 June 2026 |title=Widnes Vikings sign Danny Richardson in surprise move |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-vikings-sign-danny-richardson-in-surprise-move |access-date=31 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the England Knights Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>

In 2019 he was selected for the England Knights against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
